title: Glochidion poilanei
intro: Glochidion poilanei, a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, is a woody climber native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Glochidion poilanei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin care applications.
taxonomy: Glochidion poilanei is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is a part of the tribe Phyllantheae. The classification of Glochidion poilanei is as follows: family - Phyllanthaceae, tribe - Phyllantheae, genus - Glochidion. Glochidion poilanei is also a member of the order Malpighiales. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
